Paul's teachings on the interdependency of Church members are particularly relevant to the building of these special communities. Amid our diverse talents, backgrounds, and personalities, the eye cannot say to the hand, "I have no need of thee" (1 Corinthians 12:21). The concert pianist cannot say to the lowly piano tuner, "I have no need of thee." The pianist's great music would not be possible on an untuned piano. Correspondingly, the tuner is unlikely to be able to play the piano superbly.…

The advancement of the cause of God and the building up of Zion is as much one man's business as another's. The only difference is that one is called to fulfill one duty and another duty. Paul's teachings on the interdependency of Church members are particularly relevant to the building of these special communities. Amid our diverse talents, backgrounds, and personalities, the eye cannot say to the hand, "I have no need of thee" (1 Corinthians 12:21). The concert pianist cannot say to the lowly piano tuner, "I have no need of thee." The pianist's great music would not be possible on an untuned piano. Correspondingly, the tuner is unlikely to be able to play the piano superbly.…

Paul's teachings on the interdependency of Church members are particularly relevant to the building of these special communities. Amid our diverse talents, backgrounds, and personalities, the eye cannot say to the hand, "I have no need of thee" (1 Corinthians 12:21). The concert pianist cannot say to the lowly piano tuner, "I have no need of thee." The pianist's great music would not be possible on an untuned piano. Correspondingly, the tuner is unlikely to be able to play the piano superbly.…
